title: PRIAM enzyme-specific profiles

infobox.value: (PRofils pour l'Identification Automatique du Métabolisme) is a method for the automatic detection of likely [enzymes](/wiki/Enzyme "Enzyme") in [protein sequences](/wiki/Protein_sequence "Protein sequence"). PRIAM uses [position-specific scoring matrices](/wiki/Position-specific_scoring_matrix "Position-specific scoring matrix") (also known as profiles) automatically generated for each [enzyme entry](/wiki/Enzyme_Commission_number "Enzyme Commission number").[[1]](#cite_note-pmid14602924-1)

Claudel-Renard C, Chevalet C, Faraut T, Kahn D (November 2003). ["Enzyme-specific profiles for genome annotation: PRIAM"](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC275543). *Nucleic Acids Res*. **31** (22): 6633–9. [doi](/wiki/Doi_(identifier) "Doi (identifier)"):[10.1093/nar/gkg847](https://doi.org/10.1093%2Fnar%2Fgkg847). [PMC](/wiki/PMC_(identifier) "PMC (identifier)") [275543](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC275543). [PMID](/wiki/PMID_(identifier) "PMID (identifier)") [14602924](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/14602924).

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Method for the automatic detection of likely enzymes in protein sequences

**PRIAM enzyme-specific profiles** (PRofils pour l'Identification Automatique du Métabolisme) is a method for the automatic detection of likely [enzymes](/wiki/Enzyme "Enzyme") in [protein sequences](/wiki/Protein_sequence "Protein sequence"). PRIAM uses [position-specific scoring matrices](/wiki/Position-specific_scoring_matrix "Position-specific scoring matrix") (also known as profiles) automatically generated for each [enzyme entry](/wiki/Enzyme_Commission_number "Enzyme Commission number").[[1]](#cite_note-pmid14602924-1)

["Enzyme-specific profiles for genome annotation: PRIAM"](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC275543). *Nucleic Acids Res*. **31** (22): 6633–9. [doi](/wiki/Doi_(identifier) "Doi (identifier)"):[10.1093/nar/gkg847](https://doi.org/10.1093%2Fnar%2Fgkg847). [PMC](/wiki/PMC_(identifier) "PMC (identifier)") [275543](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC275543). [PMID](/wiki/PMID_(identifier) "PMID (identifier)") [14602924](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/14602924).
